diagnosis: Acute Viral Respiratory Infection (likely viral pharyngitis or influenza)

medication.name: Paracetamol (Acetaminophen)
medication.frequency_and_duration: Every 4-6 hours as needed for fever and headache, for a maximum of 5 days
medication.special_instructions: Take with food to minimize gastrointestinal upset

medication.name: Pseudoephedrine
medication.frequency_and_duration: Every 4-6 hours as needed for nasal congestion, for a maximum of 5 days
medication.special_instructions: Avoid taking with other stimulants or before bedtime

medication.name: Dextromethorphan
medication.frequency_and_duration: Every 4-6 hours as needed for cough, for a maximum of 5 days
medication.special_instructions: Avoid taking with other central nervous system depressants

non_pharmacological_recommendations.title: Stay hydrated by drinking plenty of fluids (water, clear broths, electrolyte-rich beverages like sports drinks).

details.text: Stay hydrated by drinking plenty of fluids (water, clear broths, electrolyte-rich beverages like sports drinks).

non_pharmacological_recommendations.title: Rest to help your body fight off the infection.

details.text: Rest to help your body fight off the infection.

non_pharmacological_recommendations.title: Use a humidifier to relieve congestion and cough.

details.text: Use a humidifier to relieve congestion and cough.

non_pharmacological_recommendations.title: Practice good hygiene (frequent hand washing, avoid close contact with others) to prevent the spread of infection.

details.text: Practice good hygiene (frequent hand washing, avoid close contact with others) to prevent the spread of infection.

non_pharmacological_recommendations.title: Avoid smoking and secondhand smoke exposure.

details.text: Avoid smoking and secondhand smoke exposure.

medical_tests.test_name: Rapid Influenza Diagnostic Test (RIDT) if influenza is suspected based on clinical presentation and epidemiological context.

details.text: Rapid Influenza Diagnostic Test (RIDT) if influenza is suspected based on clinical presentation and epidemiological context.

medical_tests.test_name: Complete Blood Count (CBC) if symptoms worsen or do not improve with treatment, to rule out bacterial infection.

details.text: Complete Blood Count (CBC) if symptoms worsen or do not improve with treatment, to rule out bacterial infection.

medical_tests.test_name: *Reasoning**: The patient's symptoms of constant sneezing, fever, headache, and cough, following a long bus trip, are suggestive of an acute viral respiratory infection. The medications prescribed aim to alleviate symptoms (fever reduction, decongestant, and cough suppression) without targeting a specific virus, as antiviral therapy is not indicated for all viral infections and the diagnosis does not specify a particular viral etiology that would require such treatment. The non-pharmacological recommendations support the body's immune response and prevent the spread of infection.
